alloy_matches.reason: Both alloys have identical base element, alloying elements, nominal composition, and sample references.

process_matches.reason: Both processes describe the same processing route for the same alloy, with detailed notes confirming identical steps.

sample_matches.reason: Both samples share the same alloy and process references, and context text supports same microstructure.

truth.papers.A21.title: Deciphering the composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels with machine learning
truth.papers.A21.paper_scope: Investigating the composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels using machine learning to predict the existence of κ phase, (γ+κ) dual-phase region, single γ region, and κ phase volume fraction, and experimentally validating on Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C.

prediction.papers.A21.title: Deciphering the composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels with machine learning
prediction.papers.A21.paper_scope: Establishing composition-microstructure correlation in low-density FeMnAlC steels using machine learning to predict κ-phase existence, (γ+κ) dual-phase region, single γ-phase region, and κ-phase volume fraction, followed by experimental validation on Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn8al1c.alloys_notes: Measured composition: Mn 30.4 wt%, Al 7.9 wt%, C 1.1 wt%, Fe balance.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn8al1c.alloys_notes: Measured composition (wt.%): Mn 30.4%, Al 7.9%, C 1.1%, Fe balance.

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn8al1c_01.descriptio: Induction melting under Ar, homogenized at 1200 °C for 2 h, forged to 60×60 mm billet, homogenized at 1050 °C for 100 min, hot-rolled to 12 mm plate, air-cooled, cold-rolled with 75% thickness reduction, annealed at 1000 °C for 15 min, water-quenched, aged at 550 °C for 2 h.

prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn8al1c_01.descriptio: Induction melting under Ar atmosphere, homogenization, forging, hot rolling, cold rolling, recrystallization annealing, and aging for Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C steel.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn8al1c_01.processes_: As-cast 25 kg ingot; homogenized at 1200°C for 2h and forged to 60mm×60mm; homogenized at 1050°C for 100min; hot-rolled to 12mm plates and air-cooled; cold-rolled with 75% reduction at room temperature; annealed at 1000°C for 15min and water-quenched; aged at 550°C for 2h.

truth.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.p: Hot-rolled to 12 mm thick plates, air-cooled to room temperature

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.p: Recrystallized microstructure achieved; water-quenched to room temperature.

truth.structures.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.overall: Single-phase austenitic (γ) matrix with κ-carbide precipitates

prediction.structures.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.overall: Recrystallized microstructure consisting of single FCC austenite matrix with nanoscale intragranular κ-carbide precipitates.

prediction.properties.sample_fe30mn8al1c_01.mechani: Engineering stress–strain curve shown in Fig. 9b.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn8a: Microstructure of recrystallized grains, no secondary phases visible at SEM scale.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn8a: Mechanical property evaluation at ambient temperature
Yield strength 953 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 1109 MPa, elongation 42%; tested at ambient temperature with nominal strain rate 1×10⁻³ s⁻¹.

truth.computational_details.A21.simulation_met: Machine learning classification and regression, 10-fold cross-validation
truth.computational_details.A21.software_used: JMatPro v9.0 for phase equilibrium data generation; Python-based algorithms (KNN, ANN, SVM, RF, CART)
truth.computational_details.A21.validation_aga: Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C experimental validation: predicted single-phase austenite with κ precipitates, low density, and high strength matched measurements.
truth.computational_details.A21.analytical_mod: Prediction of κ phase existence (Y1), (γ+κ) dual-phase region at 500–600 °C (Y2), and single γ region (Y3)
Cross-validation accuracy: 0.98 (Y1), 0.98 (Y2), 0.96 (Y3). Top descriptors: Al, Mn, VEC.
Al found most critical for κ phase existence and single γ region.
Prediction of κ phase volume fraction (Y4)
Descriptor order for regression: ΔSmix, Fe, ΔHmix, C, Mn, Tm, VEC, Al.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Machine learning classification and regression: K-nearest neighbor (KNN), artificial neural network (ANN), classification and regression tree (CART), support vector machine (SVM), and random forest (RF) with 10-fold cross-validation.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.software_use: JMatPro v9.0 for phase balance dataset generation.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Predicted characteristics of Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C agreed well with experimental validation: single FCC phase, presence of nanoscale κ precipitates, yield strength 953 MPa, UTS 1109 MPa, and elongation 42%.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Classification of κ-phase existence (Y1), (γ+κ) dual-phase region at 500–600°C (Y2), and single γ-phase region (Y3)
Best classification performance with 10-fold cross-validation accuracy of 0.98 for Y1, 0.98 for Y2, and 0.96 for Y3.
Best regression performance with 10-fold cross-validation RMSE less than 1%.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: When Mn content is 30 wt%, minimum Al content of about 4 wt% is required to guarantee the existence of κ phase.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: For (γ+κ) duplex region at 500–600 °C, Al content range is 4.5–9.3 wt% and C content range is 0.4–1.1 wt%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Maximum Al content for single austenite zone is 11.6 wt%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: From 78,477 possible composition candidates, 1,320 fulfilled phase conditions (κ exists, single γ, (γ+κ) at 500–600 °C); further screening for κ fraction ≥ 10% and density ≤ 7 g/cm³ left 150 candidates.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Feature importance analysis: Al has the strongest effect on κ phase presence and single γ region; Mn and Fe most important for (γ+κ) region.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The study used six input features: VEC, atomic size difference (δ), enthalpy of mixing (ΔHmix), entropy of mixing (ΔSmix), melting point (Tm), Pauli electronegativity difference (Δχ); δ and Δχ removed due to high correlation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The experimental validation steel (Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C) exhibited yield strength 953 MPa, tensile strength 1109 MPa, elongation 42%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Density constraint during screening was ≤ 7 g/cm³, with final selection targeting ≤ 6.9 g/cm³ and κ fraction ≥ 11%.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: When the Mn element content is 30 wt%, an estimated minimum Al content of around 4 wt% is required to guarantee the existence of the κ phase.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The anticipated content range of Al and C required for the existence of the (γ + κ) duplex region at the aging temperature spans from 4.5 to 9.3 wt% and 0.4 to 1.1 wt%, respectively.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: A maximum Al content of 11.6 wt% is necessary to ensure the presence of a single austenitic zone.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: From 78,477 possible candidates, 1,320 candidates passed the phase-structure screening (Y1, Y2, Y3); subsequently, 150 candidates remained after performance filtering with κ fraction ≥ 10% and density ≤ 7 g/cm³.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Feature selection using Pearson correlation coefficient eliminated Δχ and δ (correlation > 0.95) to reduce redundancy.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Relative importance analysis showed Al played the most significant role in predicting single γ region and κ-phase existence, whereas Mn and Fe were most important for the (γ + κ) region classification.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Cooling rate effects: slower cooling rates result in a higher frequency and more even distribution of nanoscale κ phase; water cooling was employed in this experiment.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Density screening model suggests the validation component density for Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C is approximately 6.95 g/cm³, though experimental density was not explicitly reported.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The κ phase in FeMnAlC austenitic steels is a metastable coherent ordered precipitate; during deformation, κ-carbides are sheared by dislocations on the {111}〈110〉 slip system or bypassed.
